原文: Imagine a child standing on a diving board four feet high and asking himself the question:"Should I jump?"This is what motivation① or the lack of it can do. Motivation and goal setting② are the two sides of the same coin. G. Without motivation, you can neither set a goal nor reach it. Like the child on the diving board, you will stay undecided.
翻译: 想象一个孩子站在四英尺高的跳板上，问自己：“我应该跳吗？”这就是动机或缺乏动机所能造成的影响。动机和目标设定是同一枚硬币的两面。没有动机，你既无法设定目标，也无法实现它。就像跳板上的孩子一样，你会犹豫不决。
必背词汇:
① motivation /ˌməʊtɪˈveɪʃn/ n. 动机；动力
Ⓡ motivate /ˈməʊtɪveɪt/ v. 激励；激发
Ⓡ motive /ˈməʊtɪv/ n. 动机
Ⓡ motor /ˈməʊtə(r)/ n. 发动机
② setting /ˈsetɪŋ/ n. 设定；环境
Ⓡ set /set/ v. 设定；放置
Ⓡ settle /ˈsetl/ v. 解决；定居
Ⓡ settlement /ˈsetlmənt/ n. 解决；定居点

原文: B. So how should you motivate yourself? More than that, how should you stay motivated to achieve the goal? First, you need to evaluate③ yourself, your values, your strengths, your weaknesses, your achievements, your desires, etc. Only then should you set your goals.
翻译: 那么，你应该如何激励自己？更重要的是，你应该如何保持动力来实现目标？首先，你需要评估自己、你的价值观、你的优势、你的弱点、你的成就、你的欲望等等。只有这样，你才应该设定你的目标。
必背词汇:
③ evaluate /ɪˈvæljueɪt/ v. 评估；评价
Ⓡ value /ˈvæljuː/ n. 价值 v. 重视
Ⓡ valuable /ˈvæljuəbl/ adj. 有价值的
Ⓡ evaluation /ɪˌvæljuˈeɪʃn/ n. 评估

原文: You also need to judge④ the quality and depth of your motivation. This is quite important, because it is directly related to your commitment⑤. There are times when your heart is not in your work. A. This can affect your work. So, slow down and think what you really want to do at that moment. Clarity (清晰) of thoughts can help you move forward.
翻译: 你还需要判断你的动机的质量和深度。这非常重要，因为它直接关系到你的承诺。有时你会心不在焉。这可能会影响你的工作。所以，放慢脚步，想想那一刻你真正想做什么。清晰的思路可以帮助你前进。
必背词汇:
④ judge /dʒʌdʒ/ v. 判断；评判 n. 法官
Ⓡ judgment /ˈdʒʌdʒmənt/ n. 判断；判决
Ⓡ prejudice /ˈpredʒudɪs/ n. 偏见
⑤ commitment /kəˈmɪtmənt/ n. 承诺；投入
Ⓡ commit /kəˈmɪt/ v. 承诺；犯（错）
Ⓡ committee /kəˈmɪti/ n. 委员会

原文: Another way of setting realistic goals is to analyze⑥ your short and long term objectives⑦, keeping in mind your beliefs, values and strengths. Remember that goals are flexible. E. They can change according to circumstances. They also need to be measurable. You must keep these points in mind while setting your goals.
翻译: 设定现实目标的另一种方法是分析你的短期和长期目标，同时牢记你的信念、价值观和优势。记住，目标是灵活的。它们可以根据情况改变。它们还需要是可衡量的。在设定目标时，你必须牢记这些要点。
必背词汇:
⑥ analyze /ˈænəlaɪz/ v. 分析
Ⓡ analysis /əˈnæləsɪs/ n. 分析
Ⓡ analyst /ˈænəlɪst/ n. 分析师
⑦ objective /əbˈdʒektɪv/ n. 目标 adj. 客观的
Ⓡ object /ˈɒbdʒɪkt/ n. 物体；目标
Ⓡ subjective /səbˈdʒektɪv/ adj. 主观的

原文: Your personal circumstances⑧ are equally important. For example, you may want to be a pilot but can't become one because your eyesight is not good enough. C. However, this should not discourage⑨ you. You should reassess⑩ your goals, and motivate yourself to set a fresh goal.
翻译: 你的个人情况同样重要。例如，你可能想成为一名飞行员，但因为视力不够好而无法实现。然而，这不应该让你气馁。你应该重新评估你的目标，并激励自己设定一个新的目标。
必背词汇:
⑧ circumstance /ˈsɜːkəmstəns/ n. 情况；环境
Ⓡ circle /ˈsɜːkl/ n. 圆圈 v. 环绕
Ⓡ circular /ˈsɜːkjələ(r)/ adj. 圆形的
⑨ discourage /dɪsˈkʌrɪdʒ/ v. 使气馁；劝阻
Ⓡ courage /ˈkʌrɪdʒ/ n. 勇气
Ⓡ encourage /ɪnˈkʌrɪdʒ/ v. 鼓励
⑩ reassess /ˌriːəˈses/ v. 重新评估
Ⓡ assess /əˈses/ v. 评估
Ⓡ assessment /əˈsesmənt/ n. 评估

原文: You will surely need to overcome⑪ some difficulties, some planned, but most unplanned. You cannot overcome them without ample⑫ motivation. Make sure that you plan for these difficulties at the time of setting your goals.
翻译: 你肯定需要克服一些困难，有些是计划内的，但大多数是计划外的。没有充足的动力，你无法克服它们。确保在设定目标时就为这些困难做好计划。
必背词汇:
⑪ overcome /ˌəʊvəˈkʌm/ v. 克服；战胜
Ⓡ come /kʌm/ v. 来
Ⓡ become /bɪˈkʌm/ v. 成为
⑫ ample /ˈæmpl/ adj. 充足的；丰富的
Ⓡ example /ɪɡˈzɑːmpl/ n. 例子
Ⓡ sample /ˈsɑːmpl/ n. 样本
